sourceMovie.title: Hop
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Hop (2011) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Toy Story 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hop clicked for you, Toy Story 3 (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hop?
faq.answer: Try Toy Story 3, Kung Fu Panda, A Bug's Life—each shares DNA with Hop while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hop?
faq.answer: Hop (2011) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hop worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
